user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Upgrade KIT_A2G_TC387_5V_TFT (TC38x) 3-phase inverter PWM to use IfxGtm_Pwm while keeping TOM1 pins (P00.2–P00.7), 20 kHz center-aligned PWM, and 0.5 µs dead-time; initialize U=25%, V=50%, W=75% and step +10% every 500 ms with wrap-around; allow 0%/100% by setting minPulse=0; keep all clocks/timing as in reference.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate three complementary PWM pairs on GTM TOM1 TOUT11..TOUT16 (P00.2–P00.7) at 20 kHz, center-aligned with 0.5 µs dead-time. Start with duties [25%, 50%, 75%] and every 500 ms increment each by +10% with wrap-around across 0..100% inclusive.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified high-level IfxGtm_Pwm to configure a synchronized 3-channel group mapped to TOM1 with complementary outputs and DTM dead-time. Map pins using the TC38x pin map (TOUT11..TOUT16 on P00.2..P00.7). Initialize via IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig/IfxGtm_Pwm_initChannelConfig/IfxGtm_Pwm_init, start with IfxGtm_Pwm_startSyncedChannels, set initial duty via IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate, then in Cpu0_Main step duties every 500 ms. Keep GTM/CMU clocks as in the reference and set minPulse to 0 in the PWM configuration.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified GTM PWM driver is available in the template and supports complementary pairs, hardware dead-time (DTM), center-aligned mode, and synchronized multi-channel updates needed for a 3-phase inverter on TOM1. This preserves functionality while simplifying the driver layer.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_stepDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, increment U/V/W duty by +10% with wrap-around across 0..100% inclusive.

driver_changes: Replace IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl with IfxGtm_Pwm for the 3-phase inverter PWM generation.
migration_notes.context_preserved: TOM1 pins P00.2–P00.7 are retained and mapped via TC38x pin map (IfxGtm_PinMap_TC38x_LFBGA516). PWM remains 20 kHz, center-aligned, with 0.5 µs dead-time using DTM under IfxGtm_Pwm. Initial duties and 500 ms duty stepping with wrap-around are implemented using synchronized multi-channel duty updates. All clocks/timing sources remain unchanged from the reference.
